children.text: The Ruelles Verts (green alleyways) of Montréal are an approach to unused, liminal spaces

buildings which foregrounds passage, community, horticulture, and the city's art culture. Official Ruelles Verts are mobilized and initiated with the help of an organization implementing the régroupement Éco-quartier program, as well as a borough organization and/or other partners. On their

, Éco-quartier describes the goal of a Ruelle Vert (translated from French): "To green, create, and bring to life spaces that are conducive to the environment, play, and community. Local residents, organized into neighborhood committees, share common goals and values; they wish to pool their efforts and talents, improve their space and their families’ living environment, and act locally while thinking globally."
Ruelles Verts can be found throughout the island of Montréal. There is an interactive map of them
This annotation project deep maps a

walking vlog of his experience walking through a Villeray Ruelle Vert, from Rue Jules-Verre to Rue Liège, between Rue Gaspé and Rue Henri-Julien September 2nd, 2022, at 9:15AM. This project eventually intends to eventually feature an experiential, walking vlog from several ruelles verts from several neighbourhoods in Montréal, at different points in time (time of day, season).
